title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE English Language B Paper 1R - June 2019
subject: English

sub_questions.ms_text: One mark for any one of the following: Grace Miner, Joshua Williams, Ryan Hreljac.
One mark for any one of the following: bring in real-world events, demonstrate connections, ask students to offer their opinions, devise solutions, expose them to social issues.

Explain how the writer presents her advice to teachers. Reward responses that demonstrate understanding of admiration for young people, direct address, sub-headings, positive examples, and advisory tone.

One mark for any one of the following: recognises and supports young people who are doing really positive things, about the reputation of young people in society, opportunity to show they can act as responsible citizens, to show they want to take responsibility.
Identify two points the writer makes. One mark for each valid point. (Point 1)
Identify two points the writer makes. One mark for each valid point. (Point 2)

Explain how the writer conveys her belief that young people can make a difference. Reward responses that demonstrate understanding of the Diana Award, use of authenticity, pronouns, repetition, and optimistic ending.
Compare how the writers of Text One and Text Two present ideas and perspectives about empowering students / young people. Support your answer with examples from both texts.

Write a letter to a friend about different types of organisations or campaigns, how to take part, and why it is sometimes difficult to take part. A suitable register for a letter to a friend should be adopted.
‘One person can start a campaign, but you need numbers to influence people.’ To what extent do you agree with this?
Write a story (true or imaginary) entitled ‘The Gift’.
Describe a place you have visited that you will never forget.
